headline.headline_text: Source-conditional Hold at $66.20 (close 2026-06-11, S4): probability-weighted 12-month expected return -2.5% to base-case PT $66.23 (FY27E EPS $3.58 [S4: consensus FY27E ~$3.84 at higher copper] x P/E 18.5x); scenario-weighted range [-44.4%, +58.6%]. This is a fully-valued-at-the-high call on a peak-cycle commodity: FCX is a high-quality copper leader, but the stock is at its 52-week high (~$66, +55% over 1yr) on near-record copper (~$6.19/lb COMEX, S4 - ~40-55% above a $4.00-4.50/lb mid-cycle anchor), trades ABOVE its own ~7.8x 5-yr EV/EBITDA average on peak earnings, and consensus price targets sit only ~+4-6% above spot (median $70, S4) despite 83% Buy ratings - the easy upside is gone. CONDITIONAL on (i) the copper price holding near spot (the dominant S4 driver) AND (ii) the Grasberg block-cave recovering on the revised 2026-2027 ramp after the September-2025 fatal mud-rush. If copper falls <$4.75/lb sustained OR the FY2026 Grasberg/consolidated copper guide is cut again <3.0 Blb OR unit cash cost guide rises >$2.10/lb, the bear branch dominates (~30-45% downside on the operating + multiple double-derate); if copper holds >$6.25/lb on the structural deficit AND Grasberg fully recovers in 2027, upside re-opens. The structural copper-deficit tail keeps this a Hold, not a Sell - but we would not chase at the high; the better entry is a copper dip or a Grasberg de-risk.

recommendation.rationale_one_paragraph: Source-conditional (cautious) Hold on FCX at $66.20 (S4: 2026-06-11 close). Freeport is one of the world's largest publicly-traded copper producers (~3.4-3.6 Blb/yr consolidated copper) plus a major gold and molybdenum producer, with the crown-jewel Grasberg complex in Indonesia, a low-cost US/South America portfolio (Morenci, Bagdad, Cerro Verde, El Abra), a fast-growing low-capital leaching 'virtual mine,' and downstream smelting (Atlantic Copper, Manyar). It is the large-cap US-listed leverage to the structural copper/electrification thesis. Top-3 anchors: A1 the copper price ~$6.19/lb COMEX (S4 - the dominant earnings driver), A2 FY2026 consolidated copper sales guidance ~3.1 Blb (S3, cut from 3.4 Blb at Q1'26 on the Grasberg ramp), A3 the Grasberg/PT-FI structure (FCX 48.76% economic / MIND ID 51.24%) and block-cave recovery (S2). Base PT $66.23 = FY27E EPS $3.58 x P/E 18.5x. The debate is whether peak-cycle copper at an above-average multiple is durable: copper sits ~40-55% above a $4.00-4.50/lb mid-cycle anchor (Goldman sees it declining in 2026), FCX has ~$400M of EBITDA sensitivity per $0.10/lb (so a 15-20% copper correction removes ~$3.7-5.0B of a ~$13-14B EBITDA), unit cash cost guidance has already been raised ($1.75->$1.95/lb) on lower Grasberg volumes, and ~40-50% of FCX value rides on a single asset that is state-majority-controlled, mid-recovery from a fatal September-2025 mud-rush, executing a high-risk block-cave ramp, and subject to Indonesian export-permit/divestment policy (the Feb-2026 MoU extends the license past 2041 but steps FCX's stake down to ~37% from 2042). Against that, the structural copper-supply deficit is real, Grasberg recovers in 2027 (a volume tailwind), the balance sheet is below its $3-4B net-debt target, and the performance-based payout returns up to ~50% of FCF. Probability-weighted 12-month return -2.5% with a left-skewed, fat-tailed distribution (strong_bear $36.80 / strong_bull $105.00). Earnings quality is reasonable (EY clean opinion; no restatement/going-concern), but GAAP earnings are commodity-volatile and a large minority interest (MIND ID 51% of Grasberg) sits between consolidated EBITDA and FCX-attributable cash flow. Net: a quality copper franchise fully valued at its 52-week high on peak-cycle copper, with asymmetric downside but a real structural-copper upside tail -> Hold; the actionable stance is benchmark-weight/modest-underweight and a re-entry on a copper dip below ~$5.50/lb or a Grasberg de-risk, not a chase at $66.

investment_thesis_pillars.title: Large-cap leverage to the structural copper deficit
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: ~3.4-3.6 Blb/yr consolidated copper; ~$400M EBITDA per $0.10/lb copper move (S1/S2)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: Copper is the electrification metal - grid, EVs, AI data centers, renewables - against a backdrop of declining ore grades and a thin project pipeline. FCX is the largest US-listed copper leverage: each $0.10/lb on copper is ~$400M of EBITDA, and management frames ~$14B EBITDA at $5 copper rising to ~$21B at $7 (2027-28). If the structural deficit pushes copper higher, FCX has the most torque among large-caps. The same leverage is the bear's lever on the downside.

investment_thesis_pillars.title: Grasberg recovery + low-capital leaching 'virtual mine' growth
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: Grasberg block-cave recovering through 2027; leaching ~214 Mlb (FY25) -> ~300 Mlb (FY26) -> ~800 Mlb target by ~2030 at <$1.00/lb (S2/S3)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: Two distinct growth legs. (1) Grasberg recovers from the September-2025 mud-rush on the revised ramp (Blocks 2&3 Q2'26, 1S Q2'27), restoring ~1.7 Blb/yr of the lowest-cost (gold-credit) copper by 2028 - a volume tailwind into 2027-28. (2) The leaching innovation initiative recovers copper from existing stockpiles (~42 Blb contained) at <$1.00/lb and low capital, scaling from ~214 Mlb toward an ~800 Mlb/yr target - a high-return, low-execution-risk 'virtual mine.' Brownfield expansions (Bagdad doubling, El Abra mill) add optionality.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: Grasberg full recovery ~2027; leaching scales to 2030

investment_thesis_pillars.title: Strong balance sheet + performance-based capital return
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: Net debt ~$2.4B (excl $3.2B ring-fenced PTFI smelter debt) vs a $3-4B target; up to ~50% of FCF to shareholders (S2)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: FCX is below its $3-4B net-debt target with ~8-year average debt duration and no 2026 senior maturities. The performance-based payout framework returns up to ~50% of available cash flow via a base + variable dividend and buybacks (Q1'26 dividend $0.15 = $0.075 base + $0.075 variable). At high copper this self-funds growth and returns; the discipline caps balance-sheet risk through the cycle.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: Through-cycle; payout scales with copper-driven FCF

investment_thesis_pillars.title: The bear: peak-cycle earnings + single-asset Indonesia concentration
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: Copper ~$6.19/lb (~40-55% above $4.00-4.50 mid-cycle); EV/EBITDA ~10x vs ~7.8x 5-yr avg; ~40-50% of value in state-controlled Grasberg (S4/S5/S2)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: FCX screens reasonable only if you capitalize peak copper at an above-average multiple - double-counting the cycle. On a $4.50 mid-cycle copper at a through-cycle ~6-7x, fair value is ~$40-50. The stock is at its 52-week high after a +55% run, consensus PTs are only ~+4-6% above spot, and ~40-50% of value rides on Grasberg - state-majority-controlled (MIND ID 51%), mid-recovery from a fatal mud-rush, executing a high-risk block-cave ramp, and subject to Indonesian export-permit/duty/divestment policy (stake steps to ~37% from 2042). A 15-20% copper correction removes ~$3.7-5.0B EBITDA and, with the historical peak-cycle multiple de-rate, maps to ~30-45% equity downside.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: Bear confirms on a sub-$4.75 copper print or a further Grasberg guide cut

company_qualitative.description: Freeport-McMoRan Inc. (FCX) is one of the world's largest publicly-traded copper producers and a significant gold and molybdenum producer. Its assets span the Grasberg minerals district in Papua, Indonesia (held via PT Freeport Indonesia, in which FCX has a 48.76% economic interest and the Indonesian state holding company MIND ID holds 51.24%), seven US copper mines (Morenci, Bagdad, Safford/Lone Star, Sierrita, Miami, Chino, Tyrone), and South American mines (Cerro Verde in Peru, El Abra in Chile). FCX also operates downstream smelting/refining (Atlantic Copper in Spain; the new Manyar smelter and PT Smelting in Indonesia) and a fast-growing low-capital leaching initiative recovering copper from existing stockpiles. FY2025 revenue was $25,915M (S1: SEC XBRL companyfacts CIK 0000831259) with GAAP net income to common of $2,204M (diluted EPS $1.52) and Adjusted EBITDA of ~$9.9B; consolidated copper production was ~3.4 Blb, gold ~1.0 Moz, molybdenum ~92 Mlb. Earnings are highly sensitive to the copper price (~$400M of EBITDA per $0.10/lb). The defining recent event is the September-8-2025 Grasberg block-cave mud-rush (an ~800,000-tonne inundation that caused seven fatalities, a force-majeure declaration, and a multi-year production setback - the block-cave ore body represents ~50% of PT-FI reserves and ~70% of forecast production through 2029); the phased restart runs Blocks 2&3 in Q2-2026, Block 1S in Q2-2027, with full recovery targeted 2027. A February-2026 MoU extends the IUPK mining license beyond 2041 (reportedly to ~2061) in exchange for FCX divesting an additional ~12% of PT-FI, stepping its economic interest down to ~37% from 2042. The 2024-2026 narrative is the collision of a structural copper-deficit/electrification bull case with peak-cycle copper prices, Grasberg single-asset concentration, Indonesian resource nationalism, and rising unit costs.

products.name: Copper (~3.4-3.6 Blb/yr)
products.description: Mined copper (concentrate + cathode) from Indonesia, North America and South America, plus a fast-growing leaching 'virtual mine' (~214 Mlb FY25 -> ~800 Mlb target). The core driver; ~$400M EBITDA per $0.10/lb price move.
products.pricing_model: LME/COMEX copper price (+ US Section 232 premium dynamics)
products.competitive_position: One of the largest publicly-traded copper producers; large-cap US copper leverage

products.name: Gold (~1.0 Moz/yr)
products.description: By-product gold, overwhelmingly from Grasberg (gold credits make Grasberg FCX's lowest-cash-cost copper asset). Benefits from record gold prices (~$4,000+/oz).
products.pricing_model: Gold price; by-product credit to copper cash cost
products.competitive_position: Grasberg is among the largest gold mines globally

products.name: Molybdenum (~92 Mlb/yr) + smelting
products.description: Molybdenum by-product/primary plus downstream smelting (Atlantic Copper, Manyar/PT Smelting). Diversifies the revenue base modestly.
products.competitive_position: Significant moly producer; vertically integrated smelting (Indonesia downstreaming)

company_qualitative.customers_gtm: FCX sells copper concentrate and cathode to global smelters, fabricators and traders at LME/COMEX-linked prices (with the US COMEX-LME premium an episodic, Section-232-policy-driven factor), gold to bullion markets, and molybdenum to steel/chemical customers. Indonesian concentrate is increasingly processed domestically (Manyar smelter / PT Smelting) under the government's downstreaming policy, with export permits granted in short windows. The performance-based payout returns up to ~50% of available cash flow to shareholders.

industry.overview: Copper is the structural beneficiary of electrification (grid, EVs, AI data centers, renewables) against declining global ore grades (industry head grade ~1.4-1.6% in 1990 to ~0.9% now), a thin new-project pipeline, and rising costs. That secular-deficit narrative has driven copper to near-record levels (~$6.19/lb COMEX, ATH $6.71 May-2026; LME ~$13,500/t), ~40-55% above a $4.00-4.50/lb mid-cycle anchor. The bear nuance: China is ~55% of global copper demand and its property sector (historically ~25% of world copper) remains structurally impaired, so a meaningful share of the bull case is electrification demand offsetting a China property air-pocket - and forecasters (Goldman) already see copper declining in 2026. The US Section 232 copper probe created a record COMEX-LME premium (refined copper was exempted in 2025; a June-30-2026 review may add a phased refined-copper duty 15% 2027 -> 30% 2028), a two-sided realized-price factor. Copper miners are high-beta cyclicals that historically do not sustain premium multiples at peak commodity prices.

source.ref: Global refined copper market dollarized (~26 Mt x price); electrification demand growth

peers.name: Southern Copper
peers.key_differentiator: Closest pure-play copper comp; trades at a large premium (~20x EV/EBITDA / ~39x P/E) on superior organic growth + Peru/Mexico jurisdiction; lower single-asset risk than FCX

peers.name: Rio Tinto
peers.key_differentiator: Diversified major (iron ore + copper Oyu Tolgoi/Escondida); cheaper (~7-8x EV/EBITDA) but commodity-diluted vs a copper pure-play

peers.name: Teck Resources
peers.key_differentiator: Copper-growth pivot (QB2 Chile) post-coal-spin; smaller, higher-execution-risk copper-leverage play

competitive_landscape.positioning_narrative: FCX is the large-cap US-listed copper leverage - mid-spectrum on valuation: it commands a copper-leverage premium to the diversified majors (RIO ~7-8x EV/EBITDA) but trades at a discount to pure-play SCCO (~20x), which the market rewards for better jurisdiction (Peru/Mexico vs Indonesia) and lower single-asset concentration. FCX's ~10x EV/EBITDA is above its own ~7.8x 5-yr average on peak-cycle earnings. On a 12-month horizon the dominant variable is the copper price (a 15-20% correction maps to ~30-45% equity downside via operating + multiple de-rate), compounded by Grasberg ramp/permitting execution; on a multi-year horizon the question is whether the structural copper deficit sustains a price and a fleet of growth (Grasberg recovery + leaching + Bagdad/El Abra) that the current premium-on-peak multiple already assumes. The cleaner relative-value expression is long-SCCO (quality/jurisdiction) / short-FCX (single-asset + peak multiple), accepting SCCO's higher multiple as the cost.

management.name: Kathleen Quirk
management.title: President & CEO
management.bio: President & CEO of Freeport-McMoRan (CEO since 2024; previously long-time CFO and President). Architect of the financial-discipline framework, the performance-based payout, the leaching innovation initiative, and the Grasberg recovery + Indonesia downstreaming/license strategy.

management.name: Maree Robertson
management.title: EVP & CFO
management.bio: CFO responsible for the balance-sheet (net-debt target $3-4B excl ring-fenced PTFI smelter debt), the performance-based payout, capex discipline (~$4.3B FY26), and Grasberg/Indonesia project financing.

source.ref: Modeled cash-margin allocation: Grasberg gold-credit economics make it the highest-margin segment (FCX reports geographic segments; mining gm-taxonomy adaptation)
Modeled: Cerro Verde / El Abra cash-margin proxy

entries.name: Consolidated cash margin LTM-Q1FY26 (~30.3%): revenue less site production & delivery and treatment costs (mining gm/cash-margin analog; FCX reports geographic segments, not a single GAAP gross margin)

source.ref: Modeled LTM weighted reconciliation; mining gm-taxonomy adaptation

entries.name: Segment cash margin: Indonesia/Grasberg (gold credits) ~45%; South America ~32%; North America ~28%; smelting ~8%

source.ref: Modeled segment cash-margin allocation; reconciles to consolidated within rounding

entries.name: By-metal: copper margin vs gold/moly by-product credits (gold credits at Grasberg drive the lowest net copper cash cost)
entries.value_range: Grasberg net copper cash cost ~$1.30/lb after gold credits vs ~$1.95/lb consolidated

source.ref: FCX Q1'26 unit net cash cost disclosure (gold-credit economics)

entries.name: FY27E blended cash margin with Grasberg recovered + leaching scaling

source.ref: Consensus FY27E margin proxy n=26 (assumes copper near spot)

entries.name: Marginal margin sensitivity to the copper price (the load-bearing lever)
entries.value_range: At $6.19/lb copper: ~30%+ cash margin; at $4.50 mid-cycle: ~22-24% (EBITDA -$6.8B; the bear's double-derate driver). ~$400M EBITDA per $0.10/lb

source.ref: FCX copper-price sensitivity ~$400M EBITDA per $0.10/lb (company guidance)

methods.narrative: Primary mining method per D8 (EV/EBITDA). ~7.7x (FCX's 5-yr through-cycle average) on FY27E Adjusted EBITDA $13.5B -> EV ~$104B less net debt + PTFI project debt + minority interest ~$9B = ~$95B equity / 1,443M = ~$66. Critically, at ~10x on peak-copper LTM EBITDA the stock is ABOVE its own average; the ~$66 only holds if FY27 EBITDA stays near $13.5B (copper near spot) - no margin of safety at the through-cycle multiple.

10y mine-life DCF on a mid-cycle copper deck ($4.75-5.00/lb long-run), WACC 9.85% (CoE 10.7% at beta 1.36 + after-tax Kd; equity-heavy). Base ~$58, below spot - the mid-cycle deck strips the peak-copper premium embedded in the market price and discounts the Indonesia/Grasberg single-asset + jurisdiction risk in the cost of equity.

Indonesia_segment.segment_multiple: NAV/EV at base copper less MIND ID 51% minority

methods.narrative: Sum-of-parts/NAV: Indonesia (Grasberg, net of MIND ID 51% minority) ~$40B, North America (incl leaching 'virtual mine' optionality) ~$38B, South America (net of Cerro Verde/El Abra minorities) ~$22B, less net debt + project debt. Monotonicity NI<=OP<=GP<=Revenue holds per segment (G3). ~$70/share at base copper, above comps because the leaching optionality and Grasberg recovery are credited at NAV.

Bear floor reference: a $4.50 mid-cycle copper -> ~$12-13B EBITDA at a de-rated 6.0x -> EV ~$72B less ~$9B = ~$63B equity... at a deeper $4.00 copper (~$10-11B EBITDA) and 6x -> ~$40-42/share; the 52-week low was $35.15. Not weighted; downside discipline anchoring strong_bear $36.80.

valuation.reconciliation_narrative: Comps $66 (primary, D8 EV/EBITDA), SOTP/NAV $70, DCF $58 cluster $58-70; the 5-scenario probability-weighted base PT is $66.23 (FY27E EPS $3.58 x P/E 18.5x), aligned with the comps method - essentially spot, i.e., fully valued. DCF below spot reflects a mid-cycle copper deck that strips the peak-price premium; SOTP above spot credits leaching + Grasberg-recovery NAV. Bear floor $36-42 (mid-cycle copper at a de-rated 6x) provides downside discipline and approaches the 52-week low. Methods cross-check rather than average; all three key off the copper-price assumption.

risks.title: Copper price correction (the dominant lever)
risks.description: Copper ~$6.19/lb is ~40-55% above a $4.00-4.50/lb mid-cycle anchor; Goldman sees it declining in 2026. With ~$400M EBITDA per $0.10/lb, a 15-20% correction removes ~$3.7-5.0B of a ~$13-14B EBITDA (~25%), and miners de-rate the multiple at the same time -> ~30-45% equity downside (operating + multiple double-derate). China property (~25% of world copper historically) remains impaired.
risks.mitigants: Structural electrification deficit + declining grades support a higher floor than past cycles; low-cost position; gold credits

risks.title: Grasberg block-cave ramp + single-asset concentration
risks.description: The September-2025 mud-rush (7 fatalities, force majeure) cut the block-cave (~50% of PT-FI reserves, ~70% of forecast production through 2029); the phased restart (Blocks 2&3 Q2'26, 1S Q2'27) has already slipped once and carries genuine geotechnical execution tail risk. ~40-50% of FCX value rides on this single asset.
risks.mitigants: Phased restart underway; $699M insurance settlement booked; DMLZ/Big Gossan restarted; full recovery targeted 2027

risks.title: Indonesia resource nationalism (ownership, export permits, duties)
risks.description: MIND ID (state) owns 51.24% of PT-FI; the Feb-2026 MoU extends the license past 2041 only via FCX divesting an additional ~12% (stake to ~37% from 2042). Concentrate export permits are short-dated and tied to domestic smelting; FCX has contested an export duty; the Manyar smelter (post-2024 fire) restart is pending.
risks.mitigants: License extended to 2041 + MoU beyond; governance agreement preserved through 2041; downstreaming compliance reduces permit risk over time

risks.title: Premium-on-peak valuation / cyclical de-rate
risks.description: FCX trades ~10x EV/EBITDA vs its ~7.8x 5-yr average, on peak-cycle copper earnings - double-counting the cycle. At the 52-week high with consensus PTs only ~+4-6% above spot, the multiple has more room to compress than expand if the cycle turns. Base rates: miners do not sustain premium multiples at peak commodity prices.
risks.mitigants: Below net-debt target; performance-based payout; structural-deficit narrative; SCCO trades far higher (relative cushion)

risks.title: Cost inflation + declining grades
risks.description: FY2026 unit net cash cost guide raised to ~$1.95/lb (from $1.75) on lower Grasberg volumes; industry head grades declining (~0.9% now); strip ratios rising; capex ~$4.3B/yr into the smelter + mine development. If copper normalizes, margin compresses from both price and cost.
risks.mitigants: Leaching at <$1.00/lb is margin-accretive; autonomous haulage / productivity; gold credits at Grasberg

risks.title: US Section 232 tariff / COMEX-LME premium normalization
risks.description: The record COMEX-LME premium is largely a tariff-anticipation distortion (refined copper was exempted in 2025); the June-30-2026 review may add a phased refined-copper duty (15% 2027 -> 30% 2028) or disappoint, in which case the premium embedded in FCX's realized US price deflates even if LME is flat.
risks.mitigants: US production could benefit from a refined-copper tariff; the exposure is two-sided

risks.title: Minority interest between EBITDA and FCX cash flow
risks.description: MIND ID's 51% of PT-FI (and Cerro Verde/El Abra minorities) sit between consolidated Adjusted EBITDA and FCX-attributable free cash flow; NCI distributions reduce the cash available for the FCX payout, and the post-2042 step-down to ~37% structurally lowers FCX's share of the crown jewel.
risks.mitigants: Consolidation reflects operating control through 2041; payout framework is calculated after NCI distributions

catalysts.title: Copper price (LME/COMEX) trajectory
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: Copper sustained >$6.25/lb on the deficit supports the bull; a correction <$4.75/lb sustained confirms the bear (the dominant driver). ~$400M EBITDA per $0.10/lb.

catalysts.title: Grasberg block-cave ramp (Blocks 2&3 Q2'26)
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: On-schedule Blocks 2&3 restart + reaffirmed 2027 full recovery supports the volume tailwind; a further guide cut <3.0 Blb confirms structural ramp problems.

catalysts.title: US Section 232 copper review (refined-copper duty)
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: A phased refined-copper tariff benefits US cathode (bull); an exemption/disappointment deflates the COMEX-LME premium in realized price (bear).

catalysts.title: Manyar smelter full restart + Indonesia export permit
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: Full smelter restart + export-permit clarity de-risks Indonesia cash flow; a further slip or export-duty imposition is a headwind.

catalysts.title: Leaching 'virtual mine' scale-up
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: Leaching reaching the ~300 Mlb FY26 target (toward 800 Mlb) adds low-cost pounds (bull); a stall undercuts the low-capital-growth thesis.

catalysts.title: Definitive IUPK amendment (post-2041 extension terms)
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: A definitive amended license on the Feb-2026 MoU terms removes a tail; harsher divestment/duty terms are a negative.

what_would_reverse.numerical_threshold: Copper sustained >$6.25/lb for 2+ quarters AND Grasberg confirmed on-track for full 2027 recovery (consolidated copper guide >=3.4 Blb for 2027)
what_would_reverse.observable_via: COMEX/LME monthly average + FCX quarterly guidance

what_would_reverse.numerical_threshold: Leaching reaches the ~300 Mlb FY2026 run-rate AND a US refined-copper Section 232 tariff is enacted (15% 2027)
Copper falls <$4.75/lb COMEX sustained for 2+ months (~-20% from spot; below management's $5 modeling floor)
FY2026 consolidated copper sales guide cut again <3.0 Blb (from ~3.1 Blb) OR Grasberg restart slips past Q2'26
FY2026 consolidated unit net cash cost guide raised >$2.10/lb (from ~$1.95)
Indonesia concentrate export permit lapses/not renewed OR a new export duty is imposed/upheld AND Manyar smelter restart slips past 2026
Copper de-rates the stock to <$50 (the bear case) while the structural deficit remains intact - risk/reward inflects to a Buy entry

tail_risks.name: Copper correction to mid-cycle ($4.00-4.50/lb) on China property + global slowdown
Grasberg further setback / Indonesia export-permit or duty action
NBER recession / global industrial-production downturn (copper demand)
Fed rate shock +200bp / stronger USD (commodity headwind)
Copper supercycle ($7.50+/lb) on structural deficit + Grasberg full recovery
Leaching 'virtual mine' to 800 Mlb + Bagdad/El Abra growth (low-cost pounds)

asc_606_red_flags.flag: Commodity-price volatility + provisional pricing drives GAAP earnings swings (interpretation flag)
asc_606_red_flags.evidence: GAAP net income to common swung $3,468M (FY22) -> $1,848M (FY23) -> $2,204M (FY25) on copper-price moves and provisional-pricing mark-to-market; Q1'26 EPS $0.61 on a $5.78/lb realized price. Underwrite on copper-deck-explicit EBITDA, not a single GAAP print.

asc_606_red_flags.flag: Idle-facility / restoration charges + insurance settlement (Grasberg mud-rush)
asc_606_red_flags.evidence: Q1'26 $406M idle-facility & restoration charge (excluded from unit cash cost) and a $699M insurance settlement booked; FY26 unit cost guide raised to $1.95/lb on lower volumes.

forensic_flags.vie_exposure: PT Freeport Indonesia (MIND ID 51.24% NCI); Cerro Verde (~53.6%) and El Abra (51%) minorities; PTFI smelter/refinery project debt $3.2B ring-fenced

sector_regulator_open_matters.matter: Copper import national-security review; refined copper exempted 2025; June-30-2026 review may add a phased refined-copper duty (15% 2027 -> 30% 2028)
PT-FI IUPK license (to 2041; Feb-2026 MoU for post-2041 extension via ~12% additional divestment); concentrate export permits + downstreaming policy; contested export duty

kelly_math.formula: (E[R]-Rf)/sigma^2 = (-0.025-0.0452)/0.2025 = -0.347; x0.15 conviction = -0.052 (negative -> below benchmark; Hold/modest underweight)

long_only_large_cap.mandate_constraint: Hold/modest underweight at the 52-week high on peak copper (S&P 500 FCX weight ~0.18%); add only on a copper dip <$5.50/lb or a Grasberg de-risk toward <$55

long_only_smid.mandate_constraint: Underweight; copper-bull mandates seeking cleaner exposure may prefer SCCO (jurisdiction) or a diversified major

long_short_hedge_fund.mandate_constraint: Express the single-asset + peak-multiple view as a small long-SCCO / short-FCX relative-value pair (copper-neutral, jurisdiction/quality tilt), NOT an outright FCX short into a structural copper deficit

sector_specialty.benchmark: Materials Select Sector SPDR (XLB) / copper basket
sector_specialty.mandate_constraint: Underweight within the copper sleeve on the peak-multiple/Grasberg view; overweight only on a copper dip or Grasberg de-risk

pair_trade.rationale: Long SCCO / short FCX as a copper-neutral quality-and-jurisdiction trade: SCCO carries lower single-asset/jurisdiction risk (Peru/Mexico vs Indonesia) and superior organic growth, while FCX faces the Grasberg concentration + Indonesian nationalism + peak-multiple-on-peak-copper. The cost is SCCO's much higher multiple (~20x vs ~10x EV/EBITDA) - so this is a quality/jurisdiction expression, NOT a valuation trade; size small and beta-hedge (~0.90), stop if FCX de-risks Grasberg or copper accelerates (FCX has the higher beta on the upside).

edge_decay.primary_decay_driver: LME/COMEX copper price + Grasberg production guidance + Section 232 review + Indonesia permit/duty news

consensus_variance.narrative: Consensus (83% Buy) holds FCX with PTs only ~+4-6% above spot - i.e., the Street rates it Buy but its own targets imply the stock is roughly fully valued. Our downward variance marks (i) copper at ~$6.19/lb as ~40-55% above a $4.00-4.50 mid-cycle anchor (Goldman sees a 2026 decline), (ii) FCX's ~10x EV/EBITDA as above its ~7.8x 5-yr average on peak earnings, and (iii) the historical tendency of miners to de-rate the multiple at cyclical commodity peaks; combined with the Grasberg single-asset/permitting overhang, this shifts ~2.1pp into the bear tails (a ~30-45% drawdown on a copper correction). Two-tailed (the structural copper deficit + Grasberg recovery are genuine upside) -> Hold not Sell. Sizing = magnitude 18% x probability 58% x sensitivity 20% / 10000 = 2.09.

evidence_refs.ref: FCX copper-price sensitivity ~$400M EBITDA per $0.10/lb; FY26 unit cash cost guide raised $1.75->$1.95/lb (10-K / Q1'26)
FY2026 copper sales guide cut 3.4->3.1 Blb at Q1'26 on the Grasberg ramp; MIND ID 51.24% / post-2042 step to ~37%
Copper ~$6.19/lb (~40-55% above mid-cycle); FCX EV/EBITDA ~10x vs ~7.8x 5-yr avg; consensus PT median $70 (+5.5%)

revision_velocity.breadth_note: Mixed: Goldman ->$75, Barclays init $77, but Bernstein Hold ->$59 and one FY26 EPS cut $2.81->$2.32 on the Grasberg guide. PTs net ~flat at ~+4-6% above spot despite 83% Buy. 13 Strong Buy / 6 Buy / 3 Hold / 1 Sell.
revision_velocity.pt_revisions_recent: PT median $70 (low $31 / high $85); the conservative cluster (UBS/RBC/BofA ~$46) sits ~30% below spot. Targets barely above a stock at its 52-week high.
revision_velocity.pre_print_drift: Stock +55% over 1yr to its 52-week high $72.09 (spot ~$66); next print ~2026-08-05; implied move ~+/-6%.
revision_velocity.peer_comparison: SCCO (pure-play) trades far richer (~20x EV/EBITDA) on better jurisdiction/growth; RIO (diversified) cheaper (~7-8x). FCX mid-spectrum, above its own history.
revision_velocity.verdict: Estimates roughly flat-to-down on the Grasberg guide cut; the consensus 'Buy' is belied by PTs at spot. Our caution is peak-copper multiple durability + Grasberg, NOT a near-term estimate collapse - the structural copper tail keeps it a Hold not a Sell.

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Copper corrects to ~$4.00/lb (China property air-pocket + mid-cycle reversion), the Grasberg ramp slips, unit costs inflate, and FCX de-rates to ~16x on a halved ~$2.30 EPS - the operating + multiple double-derate toward the 52-week low.
scenarios.narrative_long: A China-property-led demand air-pocket and a broad slowdown pull copper to a $4.00 mid-cycle; with ~$400M EBITDA per $0.10/lb, EBITDA falls toward ~$10-11B. Grasberg's block-cave ramp slips further (geotechnical), and unit cash cost inflates above $2.10/lb on lower volumes. The market de-rates the peak-cycle premium to ~16x as the cycle turns. EPS ~$2.30; price toward the 52-week-low region.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 16x P/E on a depressed copper-cycle EPS ~ the peak-cycle multiple de-rate; approaches the 52-week low $35.15 / bear-floor $36-42.

eps_bridge.label: Copper corrects to ~$4.00/lb (~$8B EBITDA hit at $400M/$0.10)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S1: FCX $400M EBITDA per $0.10/lb sensitivity; S4 Goldman 2026 decline

eps_bridge.label: Grasberg ramp slips further (block-cave geotechnical)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S2: GBC restart sequence already cut 3.4->3.1 Blb

eps_bridge.label: Unit cash cost inflation on lower volumes (>$2.10/lb)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S1: FY26 unit cost guide raised $1.75->$1.95/lb

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Copper normalizes to ~$4.75/lb (Goldman base), Grasberg recovers slowly, the multiple compresses to ~17x on ~$2.95 EPS - the stock works back toward the conservative-Street cluster.
scenarios.narrative_long: Copper mean-reverts toward management's $5 modeling floor / Goldman's base; EBITDA settles ~$11-12B. Grasberg recovers but slower than the revised plan; costs stay elevated. The premium partially erodes to ~17x on a $2.95 FY27E EPS - near the conservative-Street PT cluster (~$50).
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 17x ~ partial de-rate on a mid-cycle copper EPS; consistent with the conservative-Street cluster (~$46-50).

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Copper holds ~$5.50/lb (elevated but off peak), Grasberg recovers on the revised 2027 ramp, leaching scales, and FCX holds ~18.5x on $3.58 EPS - essentially fully valued at spot.
scenarios.narrative_long: Copper stays elevated (~$5.50, between spot $6.19 and $4.50 mid-cycle) as the structural deficit offsets China softness; Grasberg recovers per the revised plan (Blocks 2&3 Q2'26, full 2027), leaching scales toward ~300 Mlb, and the balance sheet funds the performance-based payout. FY27E EPS ~$3.58; the stock holds ~18.5x - roughly where it trades now (~17x FY27E) - i.e., fully valued at spot with no margin of safety.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 18.5x ~ in line with the current ~17x FY27E forward; a mid-cycle multiple on an elevated-but-off-peak copper EPS. Fully valued.

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Copper holds ~$6.25/lb on the deficit, Grasberg fully recovers in 2027, leaching + tariff benefit lift EPS to ~$4.20, and FCX re-rates to ~19.5x as the structural-copper bull is validated.
scenarios.narrative_long: The structural deficit keeps copper near spot/record; Grasberg fully recovers to ~1.7 Blb in 2027, leaching reaches its targets, and a US refined-copper tariff lifts realized US prices. EPS ~$4.20 and the market re-rates modestly to ~19.5x as the copper-leader premium is validated.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 19.5x = modest re-rate on a high-copper EPS; toward the higher-end Street PTs (Goldman $75 / Barclays $77).

eps_bridge.label: Grasberg full 2027 recovery + leaching ~300 Mlb + US tariff benefit
eps_bridge.source_tag: S2/S3: GBC recovery + leaching targets + Section 232

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Copper supercycle to ~$7.50/lb (deficit bites), Grasberg + Bagdad/El Abra growth + leaching to 800 Mlb drive EPS to ~$5.00, and FCX re-rates to ~21x.
scenarios.narrative_long: A structural supply shortfall (declining grades, thin pipeline, electrification + AI-grid demand) drives copper to a supercycle ~$7.50/lb; Grasberg recovers, the leaching 'virtual mine' scales to ~800 Mlb, and Bagdad/El Abra expansions advance. EPS ~$5.00 and the market awards ~21x.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 21x approaches a pure-play-copper premium on a supercycle EPS; tail re-rate predicated on a durable structural deficit.

eps_bridge.label: Leaching to ~800 Mlb + Bagdad/El Abra growth pipeline
eps_bridge.source_tag: S2/S3: leaching 800 Mlb target + brownfield expansions

headline.headline_text: FCX at $66.20 carries a probability-weighted 12-month expected return of -2.5% (source-conditional Hold); the call is conditional on the copper price (~$6.19/lb, the dominant S4 driver) holding near spot and on the Grasberg block-cave recovering on the revised 2026-2027 ramp - scenario-weighted range strong_bear $36.80 (-44.4%) to strong_bull $105.00 (+58.6%). FCX is fully valued at its 52-week high on peak-cycle copper (~40-55% above mid-cycle) with consensus PTs only ~+4-6% above spot; the distribution is left-skewed, but the structural copper-deficit tail keeps it a Hold, not a Sell.

memo_metadata.net_leverage_note: Net debt ~$2.4B (excl $3.2B ring-fenced PTFI smelter/refinery project debt; ~$5.6B incl), below the $3-4B target. EV-implied net debt + NCI ~$13B (EV $108B less mkt cap $95B) includes the MIND ID 51% PT-FI minority and Cerro Verde/El Abra minorities; the comps method nets ~$9B (net debt + project debt + a portion of attributable minority). Both disclosed given the large NCI.
